summary: Keystone operator packaging implementation: multi-stage Dockerfile (golang:1.25 builder, distroless/static:nonroot runtime, CGO_ENABLED=0, USER 65532:65532), Helm chart with 7 template resources (Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration), CRD copy in crds/, FluxCD HelmRelease with correct dependency chain, and comprehensive reference documentation. All files follow established codebase patterns (SPDX headers, OCI annotations, FluxCD HelmRelease settings, two-phase kustomization). ClusterRole RBAC rules exactly match kubebuilder markers. CRD in crds/ is byte-identical to config/crd/bases/ source. Documentation accurately describes all implemented behavior.

tests_checklist.item: Not applicable — declarative infrastructure packaging (Dockerfile, Helm chart, FluxCD YAML). Scope explicitly excludes Helm chart tests. No Go code written or modified.

code_quality_checklist.item: Q1: Each template handles exactly one Kubernetes resource type (Single Responsibility)
Q3: No code duplication — shared labels, names, and selectors extracted into _helpers.tpl
Q4: Clear naming — template helpers follow keystone-operator.{name,fullname,chart,labels,selectorLabels,serviceAccountName} convention
Q7: No dead code — all templates render functional resources, no commented-out blocks
P5: File locations match project structure conventions (helm/ under operator, releases/ under deploy/flux-system/)

security_checklist.item: S3: No hardcoded secrets in any file
Dockerfile: distroless/static:nonroot runtime, USER 65532:65532, CGO_ENABLED=0 static binary
Container securityContext: allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, capabilities.drop=[ALL], readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, seccompProfile=RuntimeDefault
ClusterRole follows least-privilege — RBAC rules exactly match kubebuilder markers, no excess permissions
Webhook failurePolicy=Fail (deny on webhook failure rather than allow)

architecture_checklist.item: C1: Implements exactly what was requested — all 10 tasks from the plan completed
C2: All acceptance criteria met — Dockerfile, Helm chart, FluxCD HelmRelease, CRD copy, kustomization update, docs
FC1: All tasks and requirements from the plan are implemented — nothing missing
FC2: No stubs, placeholders, or TODO comments remain
FC3: All templates render functional Kubernetes resources with real configuration
PC1: Follows mandatory patterns — SPDX headers, OCI annotations, FluxCD HelmRelease settings, two-phase kustomization
PC3: Consistent across all files — all templates use same helpers, labels, naming conventions
DA1-DA3: Documentation at docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-packaging.md accurately describes all implemented behavior

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unused templates or over-engineered abstractions — each file serves a clear purpose
Scope exclusions respected — no NOTES.txt, no values-prod.yaml, no Helm tests, no CI/CD workflows

fail_fast_checklist.item: Webhook failurePolicy=Fail ensures invalid CRs are rejected at admission
FluxCD dependsOn prevents deployment before dependencies are ready
install/upgrade remediation.retries=3 provides automatic recovery from transient failures

defensive_checklist.item: Helm conditional rendering: webhook resources only when webhook.enabled=true, ServiceAccount only when serviceAccount.create=true
Image tag defaults to Chart.appVersion when image.tag is empty
ServiceAccount name defaults to fullname when serviceAccount.name is empty

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): The CRD file in crds/ is an exact copy of config/crd/bases/ with no automated sync mechanism. The docs correctly note the sync requirement (line 488-491), but consider adding a cross-reference comment at the top of crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml pointing to the source file and a Makefile target or CI check to verify they remain identical, per the 'Flag duplicated logic blocks with sync cross-references' review pattern.

D2 (minor): No .dockerignore file exists at the workspace root. While the Dockerfile only copies specific paths, Docker still sends the entire build context to the daemon. A .dockerignore would exclude unnecessary files (tests, docs, .git) and improve build performance.

D2 (minor): The webhook configurations reference a cert-manager Certificate resource ({fullname}-webhook) via the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation, but no Certificate template exists in the Helm chart. This is correctly out of scope per the plan, but the next feature iteration should include a Certificate template to make webhook TLS functional end-to-end.
next_steps: CC-0018 (S018): Implement CI/CD workflows for building/pushing the operator container image and packaging the Helm chart to the c5c3-charts OCI registry
Add a cert-manager Certificate template to the Helm chart (or document the prerequisite) so that webhook TLS works end-to-end with the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation
Add a Makefile target or CI check to verify CRD sync between config/crd/bases/ and helm/keystone-operator/crds/
